title: TLS 1.3 Configuration and Secure Channels in Go
description: A Go service disables verification to fix staging, pins one leaf certificate forever, exposes secrets in key logs, and claims a successful handshake authenticates application users. Learners cumulatively produce TLS 1.3 client/server security profile through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace TLS negotiation, key exchange, certificate or PSK authentication, transcript binding, record protection, resumption, and closure.
Configure current Go TLS defaults, minimum versions, certificates, roots, names, ALPN, timeouts, and safe verification hooks without insecure skip shortcuts.
Model tickets, PSKs, rotation, replay, forward secrecy, and application idempotency before enabling resumption or early data.
